site.name: MiniToolset
site.tagline: Browser tools for text, data, and media—local-first by default.

home.introTitle: A collection of practical online tools
home.base64TextCardDesc: Turn plain text into Base64 in your browser (UTF-8). Nothing is uploaded.
home.base64FileCardDesc: Turn a local file into Base64 or a Data URL in your browser. Nothing is uploaded.

base64.metaDescription: Encode text or files to Base64 and Data URLs, decode Base64 and Data URLs locally in your browser.
base64.pageDescription: Switch between text encoding, file encoding, and decoding. Inputs stay in this tab unless you choose to copy or download.
base64.navEntryDescription: Text and files with Base64; decode Base64 and Data URLs.
base64.localNotice: Nothing is uploaded to our servers — processing happens locally in your browser.
base64.copyFailed: Could not copy — select and copy manually.
base64.fileTooLarge: File is larger than {mb} MB. Try a smaller file or split the content.
base64.maxFileHint: Large files can freeze the tab. This demo caps uploads at about {mb} MB.
base64.asDataUrlFile: Output as Data URL (uses file MIME when known)
base64.decodePlaceholder: Paste a Base64 string or a data:…;base64,… URL
base64.decodeErrorInvalid: That does not look like valid Base64 (check padding and characters).
base64.decodeBinaryHint: Decoded bytes are not valid UTF-8. Download the raw file instead.

base64TextEncode.metaDescription: Online text-to-Base64 encoder: type or paste text and get Base64 locally in your browser.
base64TextEncode.pageDescription: An online text-to-Base64 encoder—enter your text and convert it to Base64.
base64TextEncode.pageDescriptionHintBefore: If you need to decode Base64 back into text, try
base64TextEncode.inputPlaceholder: Type or paste your text here.
base64TextEncode.outputPlaceholder: Enter source text, and the encoded result will appear here.
base64TextEncode.uploadTextFileTooltip: Pick a plain-text file (code, HTML, CSS, JSON, Markdown, etc.); it loads as UTF-8 into the field. Not for PDF, Office, or common raster images.
base64TextEncode.uploadFileRejectNotText: Please choose a plain-text file (e.g. .txt, .html, .css, .js, .json, .md). Office, PDF, and most non-text formats are not supported here.
base64TextEncode.copyFailed: Could not copy — select and copy manually.
base64TextEncode.encodeEmptyHint: Enter some text in the field first.

base64FileEncode.metaDescription: Encode local files to Base64 or a Data URL in the browser. Client-side only; no upload.
base64FileEncode.pageDescription: Local file → Base64 or a data: URL, processed in this tab only.
base64FileEncode.dropZoneHint: Drag a file here, or click to choose
base64FileEncode.dropZoneReplace: Click or drop to replace with another file
base64FileEncode.uploadZoneFormatsLine: Supported: images, video, audio, zip and other archives, plain text and code, or any other local file.
base64FileEncode.uploadZoneSizeLine: Up to {mb} MB per file in this page.
base64FileEncode.filePreviewEmptyHint: Pick a file to see a quick preview here when the format allows it.
base64FileEncode.textPreviewTruncated: … (preview only; file is longer than shown)
base64FileEncode.textPreviewReadError: Could not read this file as text for preview.
base64FileEncode.maxFileHint: To keep the page responsive, file size is limited to about {mb} MB.
base64FileEncode.asDataUrl: Output as Data URL (uses file MIME when known)
base64FileEncode.outputPlaceholder: Choose a file and encode it to see the result here.
base64FileEncode.errorTooLarge: File is larger than {mb} MB. Try a smaller file.
base64FileEncode.copyFailed: Could not copy — select and copy manually.
base64FileEncode.supplementP1: Base64 is a way to represent raw bytes as plain text. It is not encryption — anyone with the string can recover the original data.
base64FileEncode.supplementP2: Raw output is only the Base64 alphabet. A Data URL adds a prefix like data:image/png;base64, which is common in HTML, CSS, and some APIs.
base64FileEncode.supplementP3: Your file is read and encoded only inside this tab; it is not uploaded to our servers by default (see the site privacy page for details).

base64FileDecode.metaDescription: Decode Base64 or a Data URL into a file in your browser—preview first, then download. Nothing is uploaded.
base64FileDecode.pageDescription: Reverse-convert Base64 into the corresponding file information.
base64FileDecode.pageDescriptionHintBefore: To read decoded content as UTF-8 text instead, use
base64FileDecode.pageHintFileEncodeBefore: To turn a file into Base64, use
base64FileDecode.previewSectionEmptyBody: Decode on the left first. Images, audio, video, and PDFs show here when supported; other types are still fine to save with Download above.
base64FileDecode.inputPlaceholder: Paste Base64, Base64URL, or a data:…;base64,… value here.
base64FileDecode.uploadTextFileTooltip: Load UTF-8 text from a plain-text file (e.g. .txt, .json) containing Base64.
base64FileDecode.uploadFileRejectNotText: Please choose a plain-text file for this upload.
base64FileDecode.unknownTypeHint: The file is still valid—use Download above. If it will not open, the Base64 may be truncated or corrupted.
base64FileDecode.previewBinaryTitle: No inline preview for this type
base64FileDecode.errorInvalidBase64: That does not look like valid Base64 (check padding, characters, or stray text).
base64FileDecode.errorDataUrlInvalid: This is not a recognizable Data URL. Check the data:…;base64,… prefix, or paste raw Base64 instead.
base64FileDecode.errorEmptyPayload: Decoded result is empty—there is no file to download.
base64FileDecode.errorTooLarge: Content would decode to more than {mb} MB. Try a smaller payload or use a script locally.
base64FileDecode.textLikeHintBefore: This looks like readable text. For a better reading layout, try
base64FileDecode.supplementP1: Base64 encodes bytes as text—it is not encryption. Anyone with the string can recover the bytes.
base64FileDecode.supplementP2: If a downloaded file will not open, the input Base64 may be truncated or wrong; changing the filename extension only helps when the bytes really match that format.
base64FileDecode.supplementP3: Your input is decoded only in this browser tab; it is not uploaded to our servers by default (see the site privacy page).

base64TextDecode.metaDescription: Online Base64 / Base64URL / Data URL to UTF-8 text preview in your browser. For images, audio, video, or PDFs, use File & Base64.
base64TextDecode.pageDescription: An online Base64 decode tool that turns Base64 into original text data.
base64TextDecode.pageDescriptionHintBefore: To encode plain text to Base64, use
base64TextDecode.inputPlaceholder: Enter or paste your Base64 data here.
base64TextDecode.outputPlaceholder: Enter content to decode, and the result will appear here.
base64TextDecode.copyFailed: Could not copy — select and copy manually.
base64TextDecode.uploadTextFileTooltip: Pick a plain-text file (code, HTML, CSS, JSON, Markdown, etc.); it loads as UTF-8 into the field. Not for PDF, Office, or common raster images.
base64TextDecode.uploadFileRejectNotText: Please choose a plain-text file (e.g. .txt, .html, .css, .js, .json, .md). Office, PDF, and most non-text formats are not supported here.
base64TextDecode.emptyInputHint: Enter a Base64 string or Data URL to decode.
base64TextDecode.decodeOutputFailMedia: Decode failed. The content looks like {type} data. You can try {tool}.
base64TextDecode.decodeOutputFailBinary: Decode failed. The decoded bytes are not valid UTF-8; they may be {type} or other binary data. You can try {tool}.
base64TextDecode.decodeOutputFailInvalid: Decode failed. That does not look like valid Base64 (check padding and characters). You can try {tool}.

passwordGenerator.metaDescription: Generate strong random passwords in your browser with adjustable length and character sets. Uses Web Crypto; nothing is sent to our servers by default.
passwordGenerator.pageDescription: Generate random passwords locally, tune rules, and copy in one click.
passwordGenerator.introP1: Uses browser Web Crypto; generated results stay local by default.
passwordGenerator.eachRequiredLabel: Require at least one from each selected type
passwordGenerator.copyFailed: Could not copy. Select and copy manually.
passwordGenerator.errorNoCharset: Select at least one character type.
passwordGenerator.errorEmptyPool: A selected character set is empty under current options.

icoGenerator.metaDescription: Crop a 1:1 region locally, pick sizes (16–512), optional rounded corners, then download one ZIP with PNGs per size plus favicon.ico—no upload.
icoGenerator.pageDescription: Upload and crop an image, then export multi-size PNGs and favicon.ico in one ZIP.
icoGenerator.introP1: Processing stays in your browser; images are not uploaded by default.
icoGenerator.introP2: Corner radius is a percentage of half the icon side (100% = circular). The right panel shows one live preview at the largest selected export size, scaled to fit the area.
icoGenerator.uploadHint: PNG, JPEG, or WebP. Large files may be slow; over 15 MB is blocked.
icoGenerator.uploadZoneCta: Click or drop an image here
icoGenerator.uploadZoneFormats: PNG, JPEG, or WebP · max 15 MB
icoGenerator.cropHint: The photo stays fixed; drag the blue square to choose what becomes the icon. The crop uses the largest square that fits your image.
icoGenerator.cropResizeHandleLabel: Drag an edge or corner to resize the crop (square)
icoGenerator.cropEmpty: Upload an image to start cropping.
icoGenerator.resultPreviewEmpty: No image yet. Upload an image to see the result here.
icoGenerator.errorBadType: Please choose a PNG, JPEG, or WebP image.
icoGenerator.errorRender: Could not render preview. Try another image.
icoGenerator.errorNoSize: Select at least one output size.

imageCompressor.metaDescription: Shrink image file size in the browser without uploading your files to our servers.
imageCompressor.pageDescription: Reduce weight for JPG, PNG, WebP, and more while keeping previews local.
imageCompressor.body: This page is a placeholder. The interactive compressor will ship in a later update.

imageCropper.metaDescription: Crop images to the region you need directly in the browser.
imageCropper.pageDescription: Select an area, preview the result, and export without leaving your device.
imageCropper.body: This page is a placeholder. The interactive crop tool will ship in a later update.

imageResizer.metaDescription: Change image width and height in the browser with local processing.
imageResizer.pageDescription: Set target dimensions or scale by percentage before download.
imageResizer.body: This page is a placeholder. The interactive resizer will ship in a later update.

privacy.title: Privacy & local processing
privacy.description: How this site handles your data by default.
privacy.lead: This overview applies across the site. Individual tools may add tool-specific notices later.
paragraphs: By default, inputs you paste or upload in our browser-side tools are processed in your tab. We do not receive your raw content unless a tool explicitly states otherwise.
We may collect typical server logs (such as page requests) needed to operate the site. Retention and legal bases will be documented here as the product matures.
Contact channels will be listed on this page when available.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.description: Placeholder terms of service for MiniToolset.
terms.lead: This page is a legal placeholder. Replace with counsel-reviewed terms before public marketing or payments.
paragraphs: No warranties are granted for the accuracy of browser-side tools; outputs may differ by browser or device.
You agree not to misuse the service (including attempting to disrupt infrastructure or violate applicable law).
We may update these terms; the revision date will appear here once a formal process exists.

cookies.title: Cookie policy
cookies.description: How MiniToolset uses cookies and similar storage.
cookies.lead: Placeholder policy. Expand when analytics, auth, or cross-site embeds are introduced.
paragraphs: Essential cookies (if any) are used only to operate the site, such as remembering UI preferences you explicitly save.
We do not run third-party advertising cookies in this baseline. Revisit this page before enabling ads or trackers.
Use your browser settings to block or delete cookies; some features may stop working if you do.
